title: The Three Pillars of Object-Oriented Programming
description: A visual representation of the three core OOP principles and their relationships to building modular, reusable, and secure Java programs.
relationships: Encapsulation bundles data and methods into a single unit (Class), Inheritance creates hierarchies by extending classes, and Polymorphism allows inherited methods to be overridden for specific behavior, all working together to model real-world entities efficiently.
